text: This seems to be the sense of the Latin. Some think that in the Hebrew text the ‘hands’ of the two chiefs are simply their persons.
This circumstance has not been mentioned in our narrative.

The word here used is that which describes part of the high priest’s vesture in Ex. 28. Some think it means a garment in this passage too; others, that it was a gold effigy, or the gold sheath surrounding an effigy of wood.
